concept: Tell / Recount / Give an Account to Someone
description: To tell: recounting news, a story, what happened — speech directed at a listener with content to deliver. Sits between 'haolu' (speak, the general act) and 'lophae' (storyteller, the role): telling is speaking that carries an account to someone. Reported content takes the 'mena…meno' frame.
sound_symbolism: The flowing 'sha' is the voice underway, and 'ne' settles the account into its listener — the same grounding syllable that ends 'shane' begins the proper-name particle, fittingly: telling is always telling *someone*.
grammatical_notes: With recipient and content: 'shia wei mia nophi to shane.' (They told me a story.) With an embedded report: 'shia mena lopia towe nai meno to shane.' (They told that the child was well.) Contrast 'haolu' (speak) which needs no recipient.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Telling is speech with a receiver held in mind — the grammar's dative makes the listener explicit, and explicit listeners are treated more carefully.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: News, weather, and warning traveled by telling long before writing; the teller's accuracy was the village's safety.

semantic_domains.community: How news and stories move between people
